question: The mural in high resolution
answer: The detailed scan of the mural, framed wide and straight on. Every tessera carries its own texture at this resolution.

question: Approach the wall
answer: The camera moves in toward the centre of the mural. Same-object steps interpolate per scroll frame, so the push-in is smooth.

question: The serpentine band
answer: Close on the green band running through the centre. Individual tesserae and the grout between them are now distinct.

question: The heart mosaic
answer: In on the heart with its two figures — the cut and colour of each small tile reads clearly at this range.

question: A painted face
answer: Closer still, onto one of the painted portraits set into the wall. We are well past the point a flat photograph would dissolve.

question: A photograph in the wall
answer: A framed photograph embedded in the mosaic — the scene inside it, and the wooden frame around it, are both legible.

question: Into the tesserae
answer: Right down among the tiles beside the inset — single tesserae fill the frame, each with its own chipped edge and glaze.

question: The lower courses
answer: Pulling back and dropping to the base — the red and water-blue courses near the foot of the mural.

question: Neil Armstrong's suit
answer: A different object slides in over the mural — the high-resolution scan of an astronaut's suit. Across the boundary the camera freezes and the suit rises at its own authored angle.

question: The helmet and visor
answer: In close on the helmet: the gold visor and its reflections resolve cleanly at this resolution.

question: The chest control panel
answer: The remote control unit on the chest — connectors, labels and webbing are all distinct.

question: Gloves and waist
answer: Lower still, to the gloves and the waist fittings. Same object, so the camera interpolates per scroll frame.

question: Around to the backpack
answer: Orbiting around to the life-support backpack — the camera takes the shortest path around the figure.

question: Pull back
answer: Pulling back out to take in the whole suit again from the other side, ending the sequence.
